# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cicd security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD workflow with GitHub Actions and PR comments 
on: [pull_request]   # Trigger on pull request events only   
jobs:    
  ${{ matrix.os }}-${{ matrix.node-version }} :     
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}      
    strategy:         
        matrix:          
            node-version: [12, 14]         # Run tests for these Node versions  
            os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest ]     # and on these operating systems (optional)     
            
    ${{ env.matrix_run }}:      
        - name: Checkout code   
          uses: actions/checkout@v2        
          
        ${{ env.installation }} : 
            run: |  
              curl https://sh.rustup.rs | sh     # Install rust up-to date     
              $HOME/.cargo/bin/rustup default $(cat .tool-versions)    # Set the tool version to use       
          name: install Rust      
        
        ${{ env.install_dependencies }} : 
            run: |  
              cargo build --verbose     # Install dependencies     
          
    ${{ matrix.run }}:            
        - name: Run tests   
          run: echo "Running Tests" && $HOME/.cargo/bin/rustup default $(cat .tool-versions) rust test —verbose   # Execute the command to build and then execute all of your project's unit tests.
